course_name: Advanced Rust: Memory Safety and Concurrency
topic: An in-depth exploration of Rust's ownership model, lifetime annotations, and fearless concurrency patterns for building high-performance systems.

modules.title: Module 1: The Ownership Model Deep Dive
modules.module_context: Understanding the foundational rules of memory management in Rust without a garbage collector.

submodules.title: 1.1 Ownership, Borrowing, and Slices
submodules.content_context: Explain the rules of ownership, mutable vs immutable borrowing, and how slices provide safe views into memory.

submodules.title: 1.2 Advanced Lifetimes
submodules.content_context: Cover lifetime elision rules, explicit annotations, and lifetime bounds in generic structs.

modules.title: Module 2: Fearless Concurrency
modules.module_context: Patterns for writing safe concurrent code using standard library primitives.

submodules.title: 2.1 Threads and Message Passing
submodules.content_context: Using std::thread and mpsc channels for safe communication between threads.

submodules.title: 2.2 Shared-State Concurrency
submodules.content_context: Using Mutex, RwLock, and Arc to safely share state across thread boundaries.
